metadata.title: Korean Font Information
metadata.description: Information and licensing details for the KoPub Batang and Pretendard Korean fonts bundled with CrossPoint Reader Korean Edition.
metadata.ogDescription: Information about the KoPub Batang and Pretendard Korean fonts bundled with CrossPoint Reader Korean Edition.

hero.title: Korean Font Information
hero.subtitle: Font details and technical specifications applied to the CrossPoint Reader Korean Edition.

overview.title: Overview
overview.intro: The following fonts are applied for Korean support:
overview.epubDesc: KoPub Batang - supports Korean, English, and Hanja

unicodeRanges.title: Supported Unicode Ranges
unicodeRanges.intro: The KoPub Batang font supports the following Unicode ranges:

intro.before: You can convert TTF/OTF fonts into the

webConverter.title: Web Font Converter
webConverter.description: Convert fonts directly in your browser. Choose from various Unicode ranges and view a real-time preview.

usage.title: How to Use Converted Fonts
usage.step2: Select the desired Unicode ranges (Korean, Chinese, Japanese, etc.).
usage.step3: Set the font size and bit depth, then convert.

step4.middle2: on the SD card or to the root

usage.step5: On the device, choose the font in Settings > Font Settings.

limitations.title: Supported Font Formats and Limitations
limitations.variableFontsDesc: - the variable font interface is not currently implemented in FreeType WASM.
limitations.colorFontsDesc: - color-layer fonts render in monochrome only.
limitations.bitmapFontsDesc: - pure bitmap fonts without scalable outlines cannot be converted.
limitations.note: Most common Korean fonts (Nanum Gothic, Malgun Gothic, Noto Sans CJK, etc.) convert without issue.

pythonConversion.title: Advanced: Python Conversion
pythonConversion.intro: If you need more Unicode ranges or finer control, you can use a Python script.

info.title: Unicode Ranges
info.description: By default, the Python script covers a broad set of Unicode ranges including Hangul, CJK ideographs, Japanese kana (Hiragana/Katakana), Latin extensions, and mathematical symbols. Characters without a matching glyph in the source font are skipped automatically.

kopub.title: KoPub Batang
kopub.providerSuffix: typeface. Intellectual property is held by the Ministry of Culture, Sports and Tourism and the Korean Publishers Association.

items.free: Free for the publishing industry, businesses, and individuals without a separate licensing process
items.commercial: Commercial use permitted in printed books, e-books, printed materials, advertising, and online media
items.noModify: Modification or alteration of the font requires prior approval
items.noResale: Resale or transfer of the font itself for profit is prohibited

kopub.termsBefore: For the exact terms of use, please refer to the

pretendard.title: Pretendard
pretendard.suffix: - an open-source typeface (SIL Open Font License).
